last_context: Architecture evolution update (2026-06-24): Added W. Agent Skills (11 built-in + community, progressive disclosure, skill_load). Redesigned tool surface to 9 tools matching Anthropic schemas (bash workspace-isolated, text_editor with view/create/str_replace/insert, web_search, web_fetch, memory SQLite-backed, wiki_search, skill_load, ask_user_input, present_files). WebView2 artifacts panel (separate from WPF chat). present_files bridges workspace to artifacts. Deep Research as skill. Memory tool replaces N12 _memory.md. Per-chat toolbar: Skills dropdown, Memory toggle, 9-tool configuration. Updated: feature-inventory.md, features/agent-skills.md (new), features/tool-use-agents.md, features/artifacts-side-panel.md, features/personal-wiki.md, features/windows-os-integration.md, features/settings-configuration.md, data/memory-entry.md (new), data/skill.md (new), data/artifact.md, screens/studio-chat.md, screens/settings.md, edge-cases.md, vision-summary.md.

flagged_concerns: Auto-Summarize context overflow requires separate API call costing tokens
Text completion APIs being deprecated by some providers
WebView2 runtime dependency adds ~100MB to install size
bash tool security — workspace isolation is app-enforced, not OS-level sandbox
WebSocket server security model needs review
Global keyboard hooks may trigger antivirus false positives
VACUUM requires temporary free disk space equal to database size
Rich text bidirectional text rendering complexity
Google Cloud Storage dependency for backups
Locked chat encryption — permanent lockout risk must be communicated
Git wiki version control — GitHub token storage requires DPAPI encryption
Model comparison — per-panel multi-turn conversations = N independent API streams
Skill dependency management — 11 skills require Python/Node.js/LibreOffice none bundled
Workspace isolation is app-enforced not OS sandbox
present_files auto-copy from workspace may cause UI pauses for large files
Memory tool separate from wiki — two knowledge persistence mechanisms risk of user confusion
